# Copilot instructions — SerialCommunication

Purpose: concise guidance so future Copilot sessions understand how this repo is built, structured, and how serial commands behave.

## Quick build / run commands
- Windows desktop UI (Visual Studio / MSBuild):
  - Build solution: msbuild SerialCommunication.slnx
  - Or open the solution in Visual Studio and build/run the "SerialCommunication" project.
- Arduino sketch (firmware):
  - Use the Arduino IDE or arduino-cli. Example (replace <fqbn> and <port>):
    - Compile: arduino-cli compile --fqbn <fqbn> ./SerialCommunication
    - Upload:  arduino-cli upload -p <PORT> --fqbn <fqbn> ./SerialCommunication
  - Default baudrate used by the sketch: 115200
- Tests / lint: none present in this repository.

How to run a single flow: upload the Arduino sketch to your board (see upload command), open the Windows app and connect to the board at 115200 baud, then use the app UI or type serial commands on the port.

## High-level architecture
- Two main components:
  1) Firmware (root-level files):
     - SerialCommunication.ino — Arduino sketch: registers serial commands and implements handlers (set, toggle, get, ping, help, debug).
     - SerialCommand.h / SerialCommand.cpp — small command parser/tokenizer library used by the sketch.
     - analog.c — low-level analog read helper included by the sketch.
  2) Desktop application (SerialCommunication/):
     - Windows Forms app (Form1.cs, Designer, Program.cs) provides a GUI to control pins and PWM over a serial connection.
- The app enumerates COM ports and exposes controls mapped to the commands implemented in the sketch.

## Key repository conventions & notes (important for automation)
- Serial protocol
  - Commands are space-separated tokens, terminated by newline (construct sets term='\n'). Examples:
    - set d2 high
    - set pwm9 128
    - toggle d3
    - get d4
    - get a0
    - ping
    - debug
  - The sketch prints human-readable replies and uses F() flash-string macros.
- Pin ranges enforced in firmware (see handlers):
  - set/toggle: digital pins d2..d4
  - get digital: d2..d7
  - PWM: pwm9..pwm11 (0..255)
  - analog read: a0..a5
- Parser limits and debug:
  - SERIALCOMMANDBUFFER = 32, MAXSERIALCOMMANDS = 10. When extending commands, increase MAXSERIALCOMMANDS.
  - To enable verbose parser debug, comment out the `#undef SERIALCOMMANDDEBUG` in SerialCommand.h.
  - Note: header comments mention a default terminator of '\r', but SerialCommand constructor sets term='\n' — rely on the constructor behavior when automating tests.
- Baudrate and framing: default 115200, other serial settings controlled in the Windows app UI (Designer shows parity/databits/stopbits options).

## Where to look for changes
- Modify command semantics or ranges in: SerialCommunication.ino
- Parser behavior: SerialCommand.{h,cpp}
- Desktop UI behavior and serial client: SerialCommunication\Form1.cs (+ Designer)
